strings.description: Unique string values returned or accepted by this schema

suppressed_alarm.description: Request body for suppressing organization alarms

applies.description: If `scope`==`site`. Site and site-group targets where alarms are suppressed

duration.description: Suppression length, in seconds. Maximum duration is 86400 * 180 (180 days). 0 is to un-suppress alarms

scheduled_time.description: Time when alarm suppression starts, in epoch seconds. Defaults to now; accepted range is from now to now + 7 days

suppressed_alarm_applies.description: If `scope`==`site`. Object defines the scope (within the org e.g. whole org, and/or some site_groups, and/or some sites) for which the alarm service has to be suppressed for some `duration`

org_id.description: UUID of the current org (if provided, the alarms will be suppressed at org level)

site_ids.description: Site identifiers included in the suppression scope. If provided, alarms are suppressed for the listed sites.

sitegroup_ids.description: Site group identifiers included in the suppression scope. If provided, alarms are suppressed for all sites in the listed site groups.

suppressed_alarm_applies_site_ids.description: List of UUID of the sites within the org (if provided, the alarms will be suppressed for all the mentioned sites under the org)

suppressed_alarm_applies_sitegroup_ids.description: List of UUID of the site groups within the org (if provided, the alarms will be suppressed for all the sites under those site groups)

sw_routing_policies.description: Switch routing policies keyed by routing policy name

sw_routing_policy.description: Switch routing policy made of ordered match-action terms

terms.description: Ordered terms evaluated by this switch routing policy

sw_routing_policy_term.description: Switch routing policy term with match criteria and actions

actions.description: Policy actions applied when this routing policy term matches

matching.description: Route match criteria that must be satisfied before actions are applied

name.description: Display name of the switch routing policy term

sw_routing_policy_term_action.description: Actions applied to routes matched by a switch routing policy term

accept.description: Whether to accept routes that match this term

community.description: BGP communities to set when this term is used as an export policy

local_preference.description: Preference value to set when this term is used as an import policy

prepend_as_path.description: AS path values to prepend when this term is used as an export policy

sw_routing_policy_term_matching.description: Route match criteria for a switch routing policy term; all specified criteria must match

as_path.description: AS path values that routes must match

community.description: BGP communities that routes must match

prefix.description: Route prefixes that routes must match

protocol.description: Routing protocols that routes must match

sw_routing_policy_term_matching_protocol.description: Routing protocols that can match a switch routing policy term

sw_routing_policy_terms.description: Ordered switch routing policy terms; at least one term is required

custom_versions.description: Per-model switch firmware versions to use for auto-upgrade

enabled.description: Whether switch auto-upgrade is enabled

snapshot.description: Whether to create a recovery snapshot during the upgrade process

switch_auto_upgrade_container.description: Wrapper for switch firmware auto-upgrade settings

switch_auto_upgrade_custom_versions.description: Custom version to be used. The Property Key is the switch hardware and the property value is the firmware version

switch_bgp_config.description: Switch BGP configuration for a routing instance

auth_key.description: Authentication key used for BGP neighbor sessions, when configured

bfd_minimum_interval.description: Minimum interval in milliseconds for BFD hello packets. A neighbor is considered failed when the device stops receiving replies after the specified interval. Value must be between 1 and 255000.

export_policy.description: Export policy must match one of the policy names defined in the `routing_policies` property.

hold_time.description: Default BGP hold time for switch BGP sessions

import_policy.description: Import policy must match one of the policy names defined in the `routing_policies` property.

local_as.description: Local BGP Autonomous System (AS) number for the switch

neighbors.description: BGP neighbor settings keyed by neighbor IP address

networks.description: Network names used to add BGP groups to the corresponding VRFs

type.description: BGP session type for this switch BGP configuration

switch_bgp_config_hold_time.description: Hold time is three times the interval at which keepalive messages are sent. It indicates to the peer the length of time that it should consider the sender valid. Must be 0 or a number in the range 3-65535.

switch_bgp_config_hold_time_integer.description: BGP hold time value from 3 to 65535 seconds

export_policy.description: Export policy must match one of the policy names defined in the `routing_policies` property.

hold_time.description: BGP hold time for this neighbor

import_policy.description: Import policy must match one of the policy names defined in the `routing_policies` property.

multihop_ttl.description: Time-to-live value for multihop BGP sessions to this neighbor

neighbor_as.description: Autonomous System (AS) number of the BGP neighbor. For internal BGP, this must match `local_as`. For external BGP, this must differ from `local_as`.

switch_bgp_config_neighbors.description: Switch BGP neighbor settings keyed by neighbor IP address

switch_bgp_config_networks.description: List of network names for BGP configuration. When a network is specified, a BGP group will be added to the VRF that network is part of.

switch_bgp_config_type.description: BGP session type for this switch BGP configuration. enum: `external`, `internal`

switch_dhcpd_config.description: Switch DHCP server or relay configuration keyed by network name

enabled.description: Whether switch DHCP server or relay configuration is enabled

switch_dhcpd_config_dns_servers.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server` - optional, if not defined, system one will be used

switch_dhcpd_config_dns_suffix.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server` - optional, if not defined, system one will be used

switch_dhcpd_config_fixed_bindings.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`. Property key is the MAC address. Format is `[0-9a-f]{12}` (e.g. "5684dae9ac8b")

switch_dhcpd_config_options.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`. Property key is the DHCP option number

switch_dhcpd_config_property.description: DHCP server or relay configuration for one switch network. The property key is the network name. In case of DHCP relay, it's common for many networks to use the same dhcp relay, comma-separated network names can be used here (e.g. "net1,net2")

dns_servers.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`, DNS servers advertised to DHCP clients

dns_suffix.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`, DNS search suffixes advertised to DHCP clients

fixed_bindings.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`, fixed client bindings for DHCP service

gateway.description: If `type`==`server` - optional, `ip` will be used if not provided

ip_end.description: If `type`==`server`, ending IPv4 address for the DHCP lease pool

ip_end6.description: If `type6`==`server`, ending IPv6 address for the DHCP lease pool

ip_start.description: If `type`==`server`, starting IPv4 address for the DHCP lease pool

ip_start6.description: If `type6`==`server`, starting IPv6 address for the DHCP lease pool

lease_time.description: In seconds, lease time has to be between 3600 [1hr] - 604800 [1 week], default is 86400 [1 day]

options.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`, custom DHCP options advertised to clients

server_id_override.description:
`server_id_override`==`true` means the device, when acts as DHCP relay and forwards DHCP responses from DHCP server to clients, 
should overwrite the Sever Identifier option (i.e. DHCP option 54) in DHCP responses with its own IP address.

type.description: IPv4 DHCP mode for this switch network

type6.description: IPv6 DHCP mode for this switch network

vendor_encapsulated.description: If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`, vendor-encapsulated DHCP options advertised to clients

switch_dhcpd_config_vendor_options.description:
If `type`==`server` or `type6`==`server`. Property key is <enterprise number>:<sub option code>, with
  * enterprise number: 1-65535 (https://www.iana.org/assignments/enterprise-numbers/enterprise-numbers)
  * sub option code: 1-255, sub-option code'

switch_iot_config.description:
Property Key is the IOT port name, e.g.: 
  * `IN0` or `IN1` for the FPC0 input port with 5V triggered inputs
  * `OUT1` for the FPC0 output port (can only be triggered by either IN0 or IN1)
  * "X/IN0`, `X/IN1` and `X/OUT` are used to define IOT ports on VC members

alarm_class.description: Alarm severity class raised for input-triggered switch IOT port events

enabled.description: Whether this switch IOT port is enabled

input_src.description: Only for `OUT` ports. Input port that triggers this output port

name.description: Display name for the switch IOT port

switch_iot_port_alarm_class.description: Alarm class for the switch iot port in. enum: `minor`, `major`

switch_iot_port_input_src.description: Only for "OUT" ports, input source for the switch iot port out. enum: `IN0`, `IN1`

switch_matching.description: Defines custom switch configuration based on different criteria

enable.description: Whether custom switch matching rules are enabled

rules.description: Switch matching rules evaluated to apply custom configuration

switch_matching_rule.description:
Property key defines the type of matching, value is the string to match. e.g:
  * `match_name[0:3]`: switch name must match the first 3 letters of the property value
  * `match_name[2:6]`: switch name must match the property value from the 2nd to the 6th letter
  * `match_model[0-8]`: switch model must match the first 8 letters of the property value
  * `match_role`: switch role must match the property value

additional_config_cmds.description: Additional Junos CLI commands applied when this matching rule matches

default_port_usage.description: Port usage to assign to switch ports without any port usage assigned. Default: `default` to preserve default behavior

ip_config.description: In-band management IP configuration applied when this matching rule matches

name.description: Rule name. WARNING: the name `default` is reserved and can only be used for the last rule in the list

oob_ip_config.description: Out-of-band management IP configuration applied when this matching rule matches

port_config.description: Per-port wired configuration applied when this matching rule matches

port_mirroring.description: Port mirroring configuration applied when this matching rule matches

stp_config.description: Spanning Tree Protocol configuration applied when this matching rule matches

switch_mgmt.description: Management-plane settings applied when this matching rule matches

network.description: VLAN Name for the management interface

type.description: IP assignment mode for in-band switch management
IP assignment mode for out-of-band switch management

use_mgmt_vrf.description: If supported on the platform. If enabled, DNS will be using this routing-instance, too

use_mgmt_vrf_for_host_out.description: For host-out traffic (NTP/TACPLUS/RADIUS/SYSLOG/SNMP), if alternative source network/ip is desired

switch_matching_rules.description: Ordered switch matching rules for conditional configuration

switch_metric_scope.description: Scope level used when querying switch metrics. enum: `site`, `switch`

switch_metric_type.description: Switch metric type requested for active port summaries. enum: `active_ports_summary`

switch_metrics_active_ports_summary_deta.description: Port counts used by the active-port switch metric

active_port_count.description: Number of active ports observed across evaluated switches

total_port_count.description: Total number of ports evaluated for the active-port metric

switch_metrics_compliance_major_version.description: Version compliance grouping for one switch model

major_count.description: Number of switches in this software version grouping

major_version.description: Software version represented by this compliance grouping

model.description: Switch model represented by this compliance grouping

system_names.description: Switch system names included in this compliance grouping

switch_metrics_compliance_major_versions.description: Software version compliance groupings by switch model and version

switch_mgmt.description: Switch management-plane access and proxy settings

ap_affinity_threshold.description: AP affinity threshold for switch management. If set in both site settings and organization settings, the site setting value is used.

cli_banner.description: Set Banners for switches. Allows markup formatting

dhcp_option_fqdn.description: Enable to provide the FQDN with DHCP option 81

disable_oob_down_alarm.description: Whether to suppress alarms when the switch out-of-band management interface is down

fips_enabled.description: Whether FIPS mode is enabled on the switch

local_accounts.description: Local switch user accounts keyed by username

mxedge_proxy_host.description: IP address or FQDN of the Mist Edge used to proxy the switch management traffic to the Mist Cloud

mxedge_proxy_port.description: Mist Edge proxy port used for switch management traffic

protect_re.description: Control-plane protection settings for the switch

remove_existing_configs.description: By default, only the configuration generated by Mist is cleaned up during the configuration process. If `true`, all the existing configuration will be removed.

root_password.description: Root password for local switch access

use_mxedge_proxy.description: Whether to use Mist Edge as a proxy for switch management traffic

switch_mgmt_mxedge_proxy_port.description: Mist Edge port used to proxy the switch management traffic to the Mist Cloud. Value in range 1-65535

switch_mist_nac.description: Mist NAC RadSec settings for a switch

enabled.description: Whether Mist NAC RadSec is enabled for the switch

network.description: Switch network used for Mist NAC RadSec connectivity

switch_network.description: A network represents a network segment. It can either represent a VLAN (then usually ties to a L3 subnet), optionally associate it with a subnet which can later be used to create addition routes. Used for ports doing `family ethernet-switching`. It can also be a pure L3-subnet that can then be used against a port that with `family inet`.

gateway.description: Only required for EVPN-VXLAN networks, IPv4 Virtual Gateway

gateway6.description: Only required for EVPN-VXLAN networks, IPv6 Virtual Gateway

isolation.description: whether to stop clients to talk to each other, default is false (when enabled, a unique isolation_vlan_id is required). NOTE: this features requires uplink device to also a be Juniper device and `inter_switch_link` to be set. See also `inter_isolation_network_link` and `community_vlan_id` in port_usage

isolation_vlan_id.description: Required when `isolation`==`true`. Unique VLAN ID used for client isolation

subnet.description: Optional for pure switching, required when L3 / routing features are used

subnet6.description: Optional for pure switching, required when L3 / routing features are used

vlan_id.description: VLAN identifier for this switch network

switch_oob_ip_config.description:
Switch OOB IP Config:
  - If HA configuration: key parameter will be nodeX (eg: node1)
  - If there are 2 routing engines, re1 mgmt IP has to be set separately (if desired): key parameter = `re1`

gateway.description: Default gateway for the out-of-band management interface when `type`==`static`

ip.description: Static IPv4 address for the out-of-band management interface when `type`==`static`

netmask.description: Used only if `subnet` is not specified in `networks`

network.description: Optional, the network to be used for mgmt

type.description: IP assignment mode for the out-of-band management interface

use_mgmt_vrf.description: If supported on the platform. If enabled, DNS will be using this routing-instance, too

use_mgmt_vrf_for_host_out.description: For host-out traffic (NTP/TACPLUS/RADIUS/SYSLOG/SNMP), if alternative source network/ip is desired

switch_ospf_config.description: OSPF routing configuration for a Junos switch

areas.description: OSPF areas configured on the switch

enabled.description: Enable OSPF on the switch

export_policy.description: optional, for basic scenario, `import_policy` can be specified and can be applied to all networks in all areas if not explicitly specified

import_policy.description: optional, for basic scenario, `import_policy` can be specified and can be applied to all networks in all areas if not explicitly specified

reference_bandwidth.description: Reference bandwidth used for OSPF cost calculation

switch_ospf_config_area.description: Settings for a single OSPF area on a switch

no_summary.description: Disable OSPF summary routes for this area

switch_ospf_config_areas.description: Property key is the area name. Defines the OSPF areas configured on the switch.

description.description: Administrative description applied to the switch port override

duplex.description: Link duplex mode override for the switch port

mac_limit.description: MAC address learning limit override for the switch port

poe_disabled.description: Whether PoE capabilities are disabled for a port

poe_keep_state_when_reboot.description: Whether Perpetual PoE is enabled; keeps PoE state across reboots

speed.description: Link speed override for the switch port

switch_port_config_overwrites.description: Property key is the port name or range (e.g. "ge-0/0/0-10"). This can be used to override some attributes of the port_usage without having to create a new port_usage.

switch_port_local_usage_dot1x.description: if dot1x is desired, set to dot1x. enum: `dot1x`

switch_port_local_usage_dynamic_vlan_net.description: Only if `port_auth`==`dot1x`, if dynamic vlan is used, specify the possible networks/vlans RADIUS can return

switch_port_local_usage_mac_auth_protoco.description: Only if `enable_mac_auth` ==`true`. This type is ignored if mist_nac is enabled. enum: `eap-md5`, `eap-peap`, `pap`

switch_port_local_usage_storm_control.description: Storm-control settings for this local port configuration

disable_port.description: Whether to disable the port when storm control is triggered

no_broadcast.description: Whether to disable storm control on broadcast traffic

no_multicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on multicast traffic

no_registered_multicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on registered multicast traffic

no_unknown_unicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on unknown unicast traffic

percentage.description: Bandwidth-percentage, configures the storm control level as a percentage of the available bandwidth

switch_port_mirroring.description: Property key is the port mirroring instance name. `port_mirroring` can be added under device/site settings. It takes interface and ports as input for ingress, interface as input for egress and can take interface and port as output. A maximum 4 mirroring ports is allowed

switch_port_mirroring_egress_port_ids.description: At least one of the `input_port_ids_ingress`, `input_port_ids_egress` or `input_networks_ingress ` should be specified

switch_port_mirroring_ingress_networks.description: At least one of the `input_port_ids_ingress`, `input_port_ids_egress` or `input_networks_ingress ` should be specified

switch_port_mirroring_ingress_port_ids.description: At least one of the `input_port_ids_ingress`, `input_port_ids_egress` or `input_networks_ingress ` should be specified

switch_port_mirroring_property.description: Input and output settings for one switch port mirroring session

input_networks_ingress.description: At least one mirror input source should be specified. Networks whose ingress traffic is mirrored

input_port_ids_egress.description: At least one mirror input source should be specified. Switch ports whose egress traffic is mirrored

input_port_ids_ingress.description: At least one mirror input source should be specified. Switch ports whose ingress traffic is mirrored

output_ip_address.description: Exactly one of the `output_ip_address`, `output_port_id` or `output_network` should be provided

output_network.description: Exactly one of the `output_ip_address`, `output_port_id` or `output_network` should be provided

output_port_id.description: Exactly one of the `output_ip_address`, `output_port_id` or `output_network` should be provided

switch_port_usage.description: Junos switch port usage template and authentication settings

all_networks.description: Only if `mode`==`trunk`. Whether to trunk all network/vlans

allow_dhcpd.description: Only applies when `mode`!=`dynamic`. Controls whether DHCP server traffic is allowed on ports using this configuration if DHCP snooping is enabled. This is a tri-state setting; `true`: ports become trusted ports allowing DHCP server traffic, `false`: ports become untrusted blocking DHCP server traffic, undefined: use system defaults (access ports default to untrusted, trunk ports default to trusted).

bypass_auth_when_server_down.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Bypass auth for known clients if set to true when RADIUS server is down

bypass_auth_when_server_down_for_unknown.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`=`dot1x`. Bypass auth for all (including unknown clients) if set to true when RADIUS server is down

bypass_auth_when_server_down_for_voip.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Bypass auth for VOIP if set to true when RADIUS server is down

community_vlan_id.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. To be used together with `isolation` under networks. Signaling that this port connects to the networks isolated but wired clients belong to the same community can talk to each other

disable_autoneg.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. If speed and duplex are specified, whether to disable autonegotiation

disabled.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Whether the port is disabled

duplex.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Link duplex mode for this port usage

dynamic_vlan_networks.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Networks or VLANs that RADIUS can return for dynamic VLAN assignment

enable_mac_auth.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Whether to enable MAC Auth

guest_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Which network to put the device into if the device cannot do dot1x. default is null (i.e. not allowed)

inter_isolation_network_link.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. `inter_isolation_network_link` is used together with `isolation` under networks, signaling that this port connects to isolated networks

inter_switch_link.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. `inter_switch_link` is used together with `isolation` under networks. NOTE: `inter_switch_link` works only between Juniper devices. This has to be applied to both ports connected together

mac_auth_preferred.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` + `enable_mac_auth`==`true` + `mac_auth_only`==`false`, dot1x will be given priority then mac_auth. Enable this to prefer mac_auth over dot1x.

mac_auth_protocol.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `enable_mac_auth`==`true`. MAC authentication protocol to use; ignored if Mist NAC is enabled

mac_limit.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Maximum number of MAC addresses allowed on the port; 0 means unlimited

mode.description: Switching mode for this port usage

mtu.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. MTU for ports using this port usage

networks.description: Only if `mode`==`trunk`. Network or VLAN names to trunk

persist_mac.description: Only if `mode`==`access` and `port_auth`!=`dot1x`. Whether the port should retain dynamically learned MAC addresses

poe_disabled.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Whether PoE capabilities are disabled for a port

poe_keep_state_when_reboot.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Whether Perpetual PoE is enabled; keeps PoE state across reboots

poe_priority.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. PoE priority for ports using this port usage

port_auth.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. 802.1X authentication mode for this port usage

port_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Native network/vlan for untagged traffic

reauth_interval.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. RADIUS reauthentication interval for clients on the port

reset_default_when.description: Only if `mode`==`dynamic`. Condition that resets a dynamic port to the default port usage

rules.description: Only if `mode`==`dynamic`. Dynamic matching rules that select the port usage to apply

server_fail_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Sets server fail fallback vlan

server_fail_retry_interval.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. Interval, in seconds. Sets the wait time before retrying authentication after RADIUS failure to reduce client flapping. Range 120-65535

server_reject_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`. When RADIUS server reject / fails

speed.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Link speed for this port usage

storm_control.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Storm-control settings for this port usage

stp_disable.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `stp_required`==`false`. Drop bridge protocol data units (BPDUs ) that enter any interface or a specified interface

stp_edge.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. When enabled, the port is not expected to receive BPDU frames

stp_required.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Whether to remain in block state if no BPDU is received

ui_evpntopo_id.description: Optional for Campus Fabric Core-Distribution ESI-LAG profile. Helper used by the UI to select this port profile as the ESI-Lag between Distribution and Access switches

use_vstp.description: If this is connected to a vstp network

voip_network.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Network/vlan for voip traffic, must also set port_network. to authenticate device, set port_auth

switch_port_usage_dot1x.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. If dot1x is desired, set to dot1x. enum: `dot1x`

switch_port_usage_duplex.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`. Link connection mode. enum: `auto`, `full`, `half`

switch_port_usage_dynamic_reset_default_.description: Only if `mode`==`dynamic` Control when the DPC port should be changed to the default port usage. enum: `link_down`, `none` (let the DPC port keep at the current port usage)

switch_port_usage_dynamic_rule.description: Dynamic port usage rule evaluated against LLDP, RADIUS, or peer MAC attributes

equals.description: Exact value that the selected source attribute must match

equals_any.description: List of values where any match satisfies this dynamic rule

src.description: Source attribute evaluated by this dynamic rule

usage.description: Port usage name to apply when this dynamic rule matches

switch_port_usage_dynamic_rule_equals_an.description: Use `equals_any` to match any item in a list

switch_port_usage_dynamic_rules.description: Only if `mode`==`dynamic`. Dynamic matching rules that select the port usage to apply

switch_port_usage_dynamic_vlan_networks.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`==`dot1x`, if dynamic vlan is used, specify the possible networks/vlans RADIUS can return

switch_port_usage_mac_auth_protocol.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `enable_mac_auth` ==`true`. This type is ignored if mist_nac is enabled. enum: `eap-md5`, `eap-peap`, `pap`

switch_port_usage_mac_limit.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`, max number of MAC addresses, default is 0 for unlimited, otherwise range is 1 to 16383 (upper bound constrained by platform)

switch_port_usage_mac_limit_overwrite.description: Max number of MAC addresses, default is 0 for unlimited, otherwise range is 1 to 16383 (upper bound constrained by platform)

switch_port_usage_mode.description: `mode`==`dynamic` must only be used if the port usage name is `dynamic`. enum: `access`, `dynamic`, `inet`, `trunk`

switch_port_usage_mtu.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` media maximum transmission unit (MTU) is the largest data unit that can be forwarded without fragmentation. The default value is 1514.

switch_port_usage_networks.description: Only if `mode`==`trunk`, the list of network/vlans

switch_port_usage_reauth_interval.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic` and `port_auth`=`dot1x` reauthentication interval range (min: 10, max: 65535, default: 3600). Set to 0 to disable reauthentication (no-reauthentication).

switch_port_usage_speed.description: Only if `mode`!=`dynamic`, Port speed, default is auto to automatically negotiate speed enum: `100m`, `10m`, `1g`, `2.5g`, `5g`, `10g`, `25g`, `40g`, `100g`,`auto`

switch_port_usage_speed_overwrite.description: Port Speed, default is auto to automatically negotiate speed enum: `100m`, `10m`, `1g`, `2.5g`, `5g`, `10g`, `25g`, `40g`, `100g`,`auto`

disable_port.description: Whether to disable the port when storm control is triggered

no_broadcast.description: Whether to disable storm control on broadcast traffic

no_multicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on multicast traffic

no_registered_multicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on registered multicast traffic

no_unknown_unicast.description: Whether to disable storm control on unknown unicast traffic

percentage.description: Bandwidth-percentage, configures the storm control level as a percentage of the available bandwidth

switch_port_usages.description: Property key is the port usage name. Defines the profiles of port configuration configured on the switch

switch_radius.description: Switch RADIUS override settings. By default, the switch `radius_config` is used; set `use_different_radius` to use alternate RADIUS settings.

enabled.description: Whether RADIUS is enabled for switch management authentication

radius_config.description: RADIUS configuration used when alternate RADIUS settings are required

use_different_radius.description: Selector for alternate RADIUS settings instead of the default switch `radius_config`

switch_radius_config.description: Switch RADIUS authentication and accounting configuration

acct_immediate_update.description: Whether immediate RADIUS accounting updates are sent

acct_interim_interval.description: How frequently should interim accounting be reported, 60-65535. default is 0 (use one specified in Access-Accept request from RADIUS Server). Very frequent messages can affect the performance of the RADIUS server, 600 and up is recommended when enabled

acct_servers.description: RADIUS accounting servers used by this switch configuration

auth_server_selection.description: Selection strategy for RADIUS authentication servers

auth_servers.description: RADIUS authentication servers used by this switch configuration

coa_enabled.description: Whether RADIUS Change of Authorization (CoA) is enabled

coa_port.description: UDP port used for RADIUS Change of Authorization (CoA)

fast_dot1x_timers.description: Whether fast 802.1X timers are enabled for RADIUS authentication

network.description: Use `network`or `source_ip`. Which network the RADIUS server resides, if there's static IP for this network, we'd use it as source-ip

source_ip.description: Use `network` or `source_ip`. Explicit source IP address for RADIUS traffic

switch_radius_config_auth_server_selecti.description: Selection strategy for RADIUS authentication servers. enum: `ordered`, `unordered`

switch_search.description: Switch record returned by device search endpoints

clustered.description: Whether the switch is part of a virtual chassis or cluster

evpn_missing_links.description: Whether EVPN topology links are missing for this switch

evpntopo_id.description: EVPN topology ID associated with this switch

ext_ip.description: External IP address observed for switch management traffic

hostname.description: Observed hostnames detected for the switch in search results

ip.description: Management IP address currently reported for the switch

last_config_status.description: Most recent configuration status reported for the switch

last_hostname.description: Most recent hostname detected for the switch

last_trouble_code.description: Most recent trouble code reported for the switch

last_trouble_timestamp.description: Time when the most recent switch trouble code was reported

mac.description: Switch MAC address reported in search results

managed.description: Whether the switch is managed by Mist. Deprecated in favor of `mist_configured`

mist_configured.description: whether the device can be configured by Mist or not. This deprecates `managed` (for adopted device) and `disable_auto_config` for claimed device)

model.description: Switch model reported for this search result

num_members.description: Number of members in the switch virtual chassis, when applicable

org_id.description: Organization that owns this switch search result

radius_stats.description: RADIUS statistics for this switch keyed by server IP address

role.description: Switch role reported for this search result

site_id.description: Site that contains this switch search result

time_drifted.description: Whether the switch clock has drifted from the expected time

timestamp.description: Time associated with this switch search result

type.description: Device type for this switch search result

uptime.description: Device uptime for the switch, in seconds

version.description: Software version currently running on the switch

bridge_priority.description: Switch STP priority. Range [0, 4k, 8k.. 60k] in steps of 4k. Bridge priority applies to both VSTP and RSTP.

members.description: Virtual Chassis members and their expected roles

preprovisioned.description: To configure whether the VC is preprovisioned or nonprovisioned

switch_virtual_chassis_member.description: Virtual Chassis member identified by MAC address and role

mac.description: Virtual Chassis member MAC address; for FPC0 this matches the device ID MAC

vc_role.description: Role of this member in the Virtual Chassis

switch_virtual_chassis_member_vc_role.description: Both vc_role master and backup will be matched to routing-engine role in Junos preprovisioned VC config. enum: `backup`, `linecard`, `master`

switch_vrf_instance.description: Switch VRF instance routing and network membership settings

aggregate_routes.description: IPv4 aggregate routes configured for this VRF instance

aggregate_routes6.description: IPv6 aggregate routes configured for this VRF instance

evpn_auto_loopback_subnet.description: IPv4 subnet used for automatic EVPN loopback addresses in this VRF instance

evpn_auto_loopback_subnet6.description: IPv6 subnet used for automatic EVPN loopback addresses in this VRF instance

extra_routes.description: Additional IPv4 static routes configured for this VRF instance

extra_routes6.description: Additional IPv6 static routes configured for this VRF instance

networks.description: Names of switch networks included in this VRF instance

switch_vrf_instances.description: Property key is the network name

host.description: If `type`==`lan_connectivity`. Hostname or IP address to probe for LAN connectivity testing

ip.description: If `type`==`arp`. IP address to resolve through ARP during the synthetic test

ping_details.description: If `type`==`lan_connectivity`. Whether to include per-ping results in the LAN connectivity test output

ping_size.description: If `type`==`lan_connectivity`. Payload size, in bytes, for each ping probe

synthetictest_device_vlan_id.description: Required for AP. VLAN ID used by the synthetic test when the target device is an AP

tx_mbps.description: Transmit throughput measured by the synthetic speed test, in Mbps

vlan_id.description: Identifier of the VLAN used by the synthetic test

ike_mode.description: Only if `provider`==`custom-ipsec`. IKE negotiation mode for the tunnel

remote_subnets.description: Remote protected subnets reached through policy-based IPsec

By default, we'll use the location of the site to determine the optimal Remote Network location, optionally, service_connection can be considered, then we'll also consider this along with the site location. This field takes a service connection name that is configured in the Prisma cloud, Prisma Access Setup -> Service Connections.

upgrade_device_rule.description: Device selection rule keyed by match criterion, with string values to match

upgrade_device_rules.description:
Rules used to identify devices which will be selected for upgrade. Device will be selected as long as it satisfies any one rule  
Property key defines the type of matching, value is the string to match. e.g:
  * `match_name`: Device name must match the property value
  * `match_name[0:3]`: Device name must match the first 3 letters of the property value
  * `match_name[2:6]`: Device name must match the property value from the 2nd to the 6th letter
  * `match_model`: Device model must match the property value
  * `match_model[1:3]`: Device model must match the property value from the 1st to the 3rd letter
 * `match_role`: Device role must match the property value
  * `match_role[0:3]`: Device role must match the property value from the 1st to the 3rd letter
 * `match_evpn_role`: Device EVPN topology role must match the property value
  * `match_evpn_role[0:3]`: Device EVPN topology role must match the property value from the 1st to the 3rd letter

utils_send_support_logs_info.description:
Optional, enum: 
    * `code-dumps`: Upload all core dump files, if any found. Uploads for all members of VC on switches.
    * `full`: Upload 1 file with output of `request support information`, 1 file that concatenates all `/var/log/outbound-ssh.log*` files, all core dump files, the 5 most recent `/var/log/messages*` files, and Mist agent logs
    * `messages`: Upload 1 to 10 `/var/log/messages*` files
    * `outbound-ssh`: Upload 1 file that concatenates all `/var/log/outbound-ssh.log*` files
    * `process`: Upload 1 file with output of show `system processes extensive``
    * `var-logs`: Upload all non-empty files in the `/var/log/` directory

webhook_alarms.description:
Sample of the `alarms` webhook payload.

**N.B.**: Fields like `aps`, `bssids`, `ssids` are event specific. They are relevant to this event type ( rogue-ap-detected). For a different event type, different fields may be sent. These don’t contain all affected entities and are representative samples of entities (capped at 10). For marvis action related events, we expose `details` to include more event specific details.

Events specific fields for other alarm event type can be found with API [List Alarm Definitions#](/#operations/listAlarmDefinitions), under "fields" array of /alarm_defs response object.

webhook_asset_raw_rssi.description:
Sample of the `asset-raw-rssi` webhook payload.


This webhook topic provides raw data from packets emitted by named and filtered assets.


Raw data webhooks are a special subset of webhooks that provide insight into raw data packets emitted by a client,
identified by their advertising MAC address (assets, discovered ble, connected wifi, unconnected wifi).  
The data that client raw data webhooks encompasses are reporting AP information, RSSI Data, and any special packets/telemetry
packets that the client may emit.

Note that client raw webhooks are the raw data coming from the client and do not contain the X,Y location data of the client.
In order to get the location data for a client please see our location webhooks. 
Clients can be identified uniquely across these client raw data topics and location webhook topic using MAC address as the Unique identifier (client identifier).

webhook_wifi_conn_raw.description:
Sample of the `wifi-conn-raw` webhook payload.


This webhook topic provides raw data from packets emitted by connected devices.


Raw data webhooks are a special subset of webhooks that provide insight into raw data packets emitted by a client,
identified by their advertising MAC address (assets, discovered ble, connected wifi, unconnected wifi).  
The data that client raw data webhooks encompasses are reporting AP information, RSSI Data, and any special packets/telemetry
packets that the client may emit.

Note that client raw webhooks are the raw data coming from the client and do not contain the X,Y location data of the client.
In order to get the location data for a client please see our location webhooks. 
Clients can be identified uniquely across these client raw data topics and location webhook topic using MAC address as the Unique identifier (client identifier).

webhook_wifi_unconn_raw.description:
Sample of the `wifi-unconn-raw` webhook payload.


This webhook topic provides raw data from packets emitted by unconnected devices.


Raw data webhooks are a special subset of webhooks that provide insight into raw data packets emitted by a client,
identified by their advertising MAC address (assets, discovered ble, connected wifi, unconnected wifi).  
The data that client raw data webhooks encompasses are reporting AP information, RSSI Data, and any special packets/telemetry
packets that the client may emit.

Note that client raw webhooks are the raw data coming from the client and do not contain the X,Y location data of the client.
In order to get the location data for a client please see our location webhooks. 
Clients can be identified uniquely across these client raw data topics and location webhook topic using MAC address as the Unique identifier (client identifier).

Following fast timers are set if "fast_dot1x_timers" knob is enabled. ‘quite-period’  and ‘transmit-period’ are set to half the value of auth_servers_timeout. ‘supplicant-timeout’ is also set when setting auth_servers_timeout and is set to default value of 10.

eht.description: If `template`==`custom`. EHT MCS bitmasks for 4 streams (16-bit for each stream, MCS0 is least significant bit)

he.description: If `template`==`custom`. HE MCS bitmasks for 4 streams (16-bit for each stream, MCS0 is least significant bit

ht.description: If `template`==`custom`. MCS bitmasks for 4 streams (16-bit for each stream, MCS0 is least significant bit), e.g. 00ff 00f0 001f limits HT rates to MCS 0-7 for 1 stream, MCS 4-7 for 2 stream (i.e. MCS 12-15), MCS 1-5 for 3 stream (i.e. MCS 16-20)

legacy.description: If `template`==`custom`, legacy 802.11 data rates allowed on this WLAN

min_rssi.description: Minimum RSSI for client to connect, 0 means not enforcing

template.description: Data rate template used to derive WLAN rate settings

vht.description: If `template`==`custom`. MCS bitmasks for 4 streams (16-bit for each stream, MCS0 is least significant bit), e.g. 03ff 01ff 00ff limits VHT rates to MCS 0-9 for 1 stream, MCS 0-8 for 2 streams, and MCS 0-7 for 3 streams.

wlan_datarates_legacy.description: If `template`==`custom`. List of supported rates (IE=1) and extended supported rates (IE=50) for custom template, append ‘b’ at the end to indicate a rate being basic/mandatory. If `template`==`custom` is configured and legacy does not define at least one basic rate, it will use `no-legacy` default values

wlan_datarates_template.description:
Data Rates template to apply. enum: 
  * `no-legacy`: no 11b
  * `compatible`: all, like before, default setting that Broadcom/Atheros used
  * `legacy-only`: disable 802.11n and 802.11ac
  * `high-density`: no 11b, no low rates
  * `custom`: user defined

wlan_dns_server_rewrite.description: For radius_group-based DNS server (rewrite DNS request depending on the Group RADIUS server returns)

enabled.description: Whether DNS server rewrite by RADIUS group is enabled for this WLAN

radius_groups.description: Map between radius_group and the desired DNS server (IPv4 only). Property key is the RADIUS group, property value is the desired DNS Server

wlan_dynamic_psk.description:
For dynamic PSK where we get per_user PSK from RADIUS. dynamic_psk allows PSK to be selected at runtime depending on context (wlan/site/user/...) thus following configurations are assumed (currently)
  * PSK will come from RADIUS server
  * AP sends client MAC as username and password (i.e. `enable_mac_auth` is assumed)
  * AP sends BSSID:SSID as Caller-Station-ID
  * `auth_servers` is required
  * PSK will come from cloud WLC if source is cloud_psks
  * default_psk will be used if cloud WLC is not available
  * `multi_psk_only` and `psk` is ignored
  * `pairwise` can only be wpa2-ccmp (for now, wpa3 support on the roadmap)

default_psk.description: Default PSK to use if cloud WLC is not available, 8-63 characters

default_vlan_id.description: Default VLAN ID used when dynamic PSK lookup does not return a VLAN

enabled.description: Whether dynamic PSK is enabled for this WLAN

force_lookup.description: When 11r is enabled, we'll try to use the cached PMK, this can be disabled. `false` means auto

source.description: Origin used to retrieve per-user PSKs

wlan_dynamic_vlan.description: Dynamic VLAN assignment settings for 802.1X WLAN authentication

default_vlan_id.description: Deprecated default VLAN ID used when no RADIUS VLAN match is returned. Please use `default_vlan_ids` instead, which can support VLAN pools.

default_vlan_ids.description: Fallback VLAN IDs, ranges, or variables used when no RADIUS VLAN match is returned

enabled.description: Requires `vlan_enabled`==`true` to be set to `true`. Whether to enable dynamic vlan

local_vlan_ids.description: VLAN IDs that should be locally bridged for dynamic VLAN assignment

type.description: Dynamic VLAN mapping method used for RADIUS-provided VLAN attributes

vlans.description:
Map between vlan_id (as string) to airespace interface names (comma-separated) or null for standard mapping
  * if `dynamic_vlan.type`==`standard`, property key is the VLAN ID and property value is \"\"
  * if `dynamic_vlan.type`==`airespace-interface-name`, property key is the VLAN ID and property value is the Airespace Interface Name

wlan_dynamic_vlan_default_vlan_id.description: VLAN ID, VLAN range or variable to use when there’s no match from RADIUS

wlan_dynamic_vlan_default_vlan_id_deprec.description: vlan_id to use when there’s no match from RADIUS

wlan_dynamic_vlan_default_vlan_ids.description: Default VLAN ID(s) can be a number, a range of VLAN IDs, a variable or multiple numbers, ranges or variables as a VLAN pool. Default VLAN as a pool of VLANS requires 0.14.x or newer firmware

wlan_dynamic_vlan_type.description: standard (using Tunnel-Private-Group-ID, widely supported), airespace-interface-name (Airespace/Cisco). enum: `airespace-interface-name`, `standard`

wlan_hotspot20_domain_name.description: Domain names advertised to Hotspot 2.0 clients

wlan_hotspot20_rcoi.description: Roaming Consortium Organization Identifiers advertised for Hotspot 2.0

domain_name.description: Advertised domain names for Hotspot 2.0 clients

enabled.description: Whether to enable hotspot 2.0 config

nai_realms.description: NAI realms advertised for Hotspot 2.0 authentication

operators.description: Operator profiles supported by this Hotspot 2.0 configuration

rcoi.description: Roaming Consortium Organization Identifiers advertised for Hotspot 2.0

venue_name.description: Venue name, default is site name

wlan_inject_dhcp_option_82.description: DHCP Option 82 injection settings for a WLAN

enabled.description: Whether to inject option 82 when forwarding DHCP packets

wlan_interface.description: where this WLAN will be connected to. enum: `all`, `eth0`, `eth1`, `eth2`, `eth3`, `mxtunnel`, `site_mxedge`, `wxtunnel`

wlan_mist_nac.description: Mist NAC RADIUS settings for a WLAN

acct_interim_interval.description: How frequently should interim accounting be reported, 60-65535. default is 0 (use one specified in Access-Accept request from Server). Very frequent messages can affect the performance of the RADIUS server, 600 and up is recommended when enabled.

auth_servers_retries.description: RADIUS auth session retries. Following fast timers are set if `fast_dot1x_timers` knob is enabled. "retries" are set to value of `auth_servers_timeout`. "max-requests" is also set when setting `auth_servers_retries` is set to default value to 3.

auth_servers_timeout.description: RADIUS auth session timeout. Following fast timers are set if `fast_dot1x_timers` knob is enabled. "quite-period" and "transmit-period" are set to half the value of `auth_servers_timeout`. "supplicant-timeout" is also set when setting `auth_servers_timeout` is set to default value of 10.

coa_enabled.description: Allows a RADIUS server to dynamically modify the authorization status of a user session.

coa_port.description: the communication port used for “Change of Authorization” (CoA) messages

enabled.description:
When enabled:
  * `auth_servers` is ignored
  * `acct_servers` is ignored
  * `auth_servers_*` are ignored
  * `coa_servers` is ignored
  * `radsec` is ignored
  * `coa_enabled` is assumed

fast_dot1x_timers.description: If set to true, sets default fast-timers with values calculated from `auth_servers_timeout` and `auth_server_retries`.

network.description: Which network the mist nac server resides in

source_ip.description: In case there is a static IP for this network, we can specify it using source ip

wlan_mxtunnel_ids.description: When `interface`=`mxtunnel`, id of the Mist Tunnel

wlan_mxtunnel_name.description: When `interface`=`site_mxedge`, name of the mxtunnel that in mxtunnels under Site Setting

wlan_portal.description: Guest portal settings for the WLAN

allow_wlan_id_roam.description: Optional if `amazon_enabled`==`true`. Whether to allow guest to connect to other Guest WLANs (with different `WLAN.ssid`) of same org without reauthentication (disable random_mac for seamless roaming)

amazon_client_id.description: Optional if `amazon_enabled`==`true`. Amazon OAuth2 client id. This is optional. If not provided, it will use a default one.

amazon_client_secret.description: Optional if `amazon_enabled`==`true`. Amazon OAuth2 client secret. If amazon_client_id was provided, provide a corresponding value. Else leave blank.

amazon_email_domains.description: Optional if `amazon_enabled`==`true`. Email domains allowed for Amazon-authenticated guest users. If null or empty, any authenticated Amazon email domain is allowed.

amazon_enabled.description: Whether amazon is enabled as a login method

amazon_expire.description: Optional if `amazon_enabled`==`true`. Interval for which guest remains authorized using amazon auth (in minutes), if not provided, uses expire`

auth.description: Guest portal login scheme used by the WLAN

azure_client_id.description: Required if `azure_enabled`==`true`. Azure active directory app client id

azure_client_secret.description: Required if `azure_enabled`==`true`. Azure active directory app client secret

azure_enabled.description: Whether Azure Active Directory is enabled as a login method

azure_expire.description: Interval for which guest remains authorized using azure auth (in minutes), if not provided, uses expire`

azure_tenant_id.description: Required if `azure_enabled`==`true`. Azure active directory tenant id.

broadnet_password.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`broadnet`. Password for the Broadnet SMS provider account

broadnet_sid.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`broadnet`. SID for the Broadnet SMS provider account

broadnet_user_id.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`broadnet`. User ID for the Broadnet SMS provider account

bypass_when_cloud_down.description: Whether to bypass the guest portal when cloud not reachable (and apply the default policies)

clickatell_api_key.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`clickatell`. API key for the Clickatell SMS provider account

cross_site.description: Whether to allow guest to roam between WLANs (with same `WLAN.ssid`, regardless of variables) of different sites of same org without reauthentication (disable random_mac for seamless roaming)

email_enabled.description: Whether email (access code verification) is enabled as a login method

enabled.description: Whether guest portal is enabled

expire.description: How long to remain authorized, in minutes

external_portal_url.description: Required if `wlan_portal_auth`==`external`. External portal URL (e.g. https://host/url) where we can append our query parameters to

facebook_client_id.description: Required if `facebook_enabled`==`true`. Facebook OAuth2 app id. This is optional. If not provided, it will use a default one.

facebook_client_secret.description: Required if `facebook_enabled`==`true`. Facebook OAuth2 app secret. If facebook_client_id was provided, provide a corresponding value. Else leave blank.

facebook_email_domains.description: Optional if `facebook_enabled`==`true`. Email domains allowed for Facebook-authenticated guest users. If null or empty, any authenticated Facebook email domain is allowed.

facebook_enabled.description: Whether facebook is enabled as a login method

facebook_expire.description: Optional if `facebook_enabled`==`true`. Interval for which guest remains authorized using facebook auth (in minutes), if not provided, uses expire`

forward.description: Whether to forward the user to another URL after authorized

forward_url.description: URL to forward the user to

google_client_id.description: Google OAuth2 app id. This is optional. If not provided, it will use a default one.

google_client_secret.description: Optional if `google_enabled`==`true`. Google OAuth2 app secret. If google_client_id was provided, provide a corresponding value. Else leave blank.

google_email_domains.description: Optional if `google_enabled`==`true`. Email domains allowed for Google-authenticated guest users. If null or empty, any authenticated Google email domain is allowed.

google_enabled.description: Whether Google is enabled as login method

google_expire.description: Optional if `google_enabled`==`true`. Interval for which guest remains authorized using Google Auth (in minutes), if not provided, uses expire`

gupshup_password.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`gupshup`. Password for the Gupshup SMS provider account

gupshup_userid.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`gupshup`. User ID for the Gupshup SMS provider account

microsoft_client_id.description: Optional if `microsoft_enabled`==`true`. Microsoft 365 OAuth2 client id. This is optional. If not provided, it will use a default one.

microsoft_client_secret.description: Optional if `microsoft_enabled`==`true`. Microsoft 365 OAuth2 client secret. If microsoft_client_id was provided, provide a corresponding value. Else leave blank.

microsoft_email_domains.description: Optional if `microsoft_enabled`==`true`. Email domains allowed for Microsoft 365-authenticated guest users. If null or empty, any authenticated Microsoft 365 email domain is allowed.

microsoft_enabled.description: Whether microsoft 365 is enabled as a login method

microsoft_expire.description: Optional if `microsoft_enabled`==`true`. Interval for which guest remains authorized using microsoft auth (in minutes), if not provided, uses expire`

passphrase_expire.description: Optional if `passphrase_enabled`==`true`. Interval for which guest remains authorized using passphrase auth (in minutes), if not provided, uses `expire`

password.description: Required if `passphrase_enabled`==`true`. Passphrase guests must enter when passphrase authentication is enabled

predefined_sponsors_enabled.description: Whether to show list of sponsor emails mentioned in `sponsors` object as a dropdown. If both `sponsor_notify_all` and `predefined_sponsors_enabled` are false, behavior is acc to `sponsor_email_domains`

predefined_sponsors_hide_email.description: Whether to hide sponsor’s email from list of sponsors

privacy.description: Whether to show the privacy policy in the WLAN guest portal

puzzel_password.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`puzzel`. Password for the Puzzel SMS provider account

puzzel_service_id.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`puzzel`. Service ID for the Puzzel SMS provider account

puzzel_username.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`puzzel`. Username for the Puzzel SMS provider account

sms_enabled.description: Whether sms is enabled as a login method

sms_expire.description: Optional if `sms_enabled`==`true`. Interval for which guest remains authorized using sms auth (in minutes), if not provided, uses expire`

sms_provider.description: Optional if `sms_enabled`==`true`. SMS provider used to deliver guest portal access codes

smsglobal_sender.description: Optional sender's number or sender ID for SMSGlobal. If not provided, uses the default number associated with the account

sponsor_auto_approve.description: Optional if `sponsor_enabled`==`true`. Whether to automatically approve guest and allow sponsor to revoke guest access, needs predefined_sponsors_enabled enabled and sponsor_notify_all disabled

sponsor_email_domains.description: Email domains allowed for sponsor email addresses. Required if `sponsor_enabled` is `true` and `sponsors` is empty.

sponsor_expire.description: Optional if `sponsor_enabled`==`true`. Interval for which guest remains authorized using sponsor auth (in minutes), if not provided, uses expire`

sponsor_link_validity_duration.description: Optional if `sponsor_enabled`==`true`. Validity duration, in minutes, for approve or deny links in sponsor emails. Valid values are 5 to 60.

sponsor_notify_all.description: Optional if `sponsor_enabled`==`true`. whether to notify all sponsors that are mentioned in `sponsors` object. Both `sponsor_notify_all` and `predefined_sponsors_enabled` should be true in order to notify sponsors. If true, email sent to 10 sponsors in no particular order.

sponsor_status_notify.description: Optional if `sponsor_enabled`==`true`. If enabled, guest will get email about sponsor's action (approve/deny)

sponsors.description: Allowed sponsor email addresses and display names. Required if `sponsor_enabled` is `true` and `sponsor_email_domains` is empty.

sso_default_role.description: Optional if `wlan_portal_auth`==`sso`, default role to assign if there’s no match. By default, an assertion is treated as invalid when there’s no role matched

sso_forced_role.description: Optional if `wlan_portal_auth`==`sso`. Role assigned to authenticated users when guest SSO is used

sso_idp_cert.description: Required if `wlan_portal_auth`==`sso`. IDP Cert (used to verify the signed response)

sso_idp_sign_algo.description: Optional if `wlan_portal_auth`==`sso`. Signing algorithm used for SAML assertions from the identity provider

sso_nameid_format.description: Optional if `wlan_portal_auth`==`sso`. SAML NameID format expected from the identity provider

telstra_client_id.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`telstra`, Client ID provided by Telstra

telstra_client_secret.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`telstra`, Client secret provided by Telstra

twilio_auth_token.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`twilio`, Auth token account with twilio account

twilio_phone_number.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`twilio`, Twilio phone number associated with the account. See example for accepted format.

twilio_sid.description: Required if `sms_provider`==`twilio`, Account SID provided by Twilio

wlan_portal_allowed_subnets.description: Guest portal CIDR subnets that clients may reach before authorization

wlan_portal_denied_hostnames.description: List of hostnames without http(s):// (matched by substring), this takes precedence over portal_allowed_hostnames

wlan_portal_facebook_email_domains.description: Optional if `facebook_enabled`==`true`. Matches authenticated user email against provided domains. If null or [], all authenticated emails will be allowed.

wlan_portal_google_email_domains.description: Optional if `google_enabled`==`true`. Matches authenticated user email against provided domains. If null or [], all authenticated emails will be allowed.

wlan_portal_microsoft_email_domains.description: Optional if `microsoft_enabled`==`true`. Matches authenticated user email against provided domains. If null or [], all authenticated emails will be allowed.

sponsorEmailTemplate.description:
HTML template to replace/override default sponsor email template 
Sponsor Email Template supports following template variables:
  * `approve_url`: Renders URL to approve the request; optionally &minutes=N query param can be appended to change the Authorization period of the guest, where N is a valid integer denoting number of minutes a guest remains authorized
  * `deny_url`: Renders URL to reject the request
  * `guest_email`: Renders Email ID of the guest
  * `guest_name`: Renders Name of the guest
  * `field1`: Renders value of the Custom Field 1
  * `field2`: Renders value of the Custom Field 2
  * `sponsor_link_validity_duration`: Renders validity time of the request (i.e. Approve/Deny URL)
  * `auth_expire_minutes`: Renders Wlan-level configured Guest Authorization Expiration time period (in minutes), If not configured then default (1 day in minutes)

wxlan_tag.description:
WxLAN Tag
  * type:
    * client: created manually (e.g. on wireless client table, when they spot a device of interest, they can create a wxlan tag for it
    * resource: created automatically when we discover a network resource
    * subnet: create automatically when a subnet is discovered
  * match:
    * wlan_id, ap_id: values are a list of Wlan / Device ids
    * client_mac: values are a list of MAC addresses
  * radius_group: this is a smart tag that matches RADIUS-Filter-ID, Airespace-ACL-Name (VendorID=14179, VendorType=6) / Aruba-User-Role (VendorID=14823, VendorType=1)
  * radius_username: this matches the ATTR-User-Name(1)
  * radius_class: the matches the ATTR-Class(25)
  * radius_attr: the values are [ "6=1" , "26=10.2.3.4" ], this support other RADIUS attributes where we know the type
  * radius_vendor: the values are [ "14179.10=1" , "14178.16=1.2.3.4" ], this matches vendor attributes and will be dynamically evaluated

created_time.description: Time when this WxLAN tag was created, in epoch seconds

for_site.description: Whether this WxLAN tag is scoped to a site

id.description: Unique identifier for this WxLAN tag

last_ips.description: Most recent IP addresses observed for this WxLAN tag

mac.description: If `type`==`client`, Client MAC address

modified_time.description: Time when this WxLAN tag was last modified, in epoch seconds

name.description: Display name of the WxLAN tag

op.description: Required if `type`==`match`; whether `values` are inclusive or exclusive matches

org_id.description: Owning organization associated with this WxLAN tag

resource_mac.description: MAC address of the discovered resource associated with this tag, when applicable

services.description: Names of services associated with this WxLAN tag

site_id.description: Mist site associated with this WxLAN tag, when site-scoped

subnet.description: CIDR subnet associated with this WxLAN tag when `type`==`subnet`

type.description: Kind of WxLAN tag and how it is populated

values.description: Comparison values for the selected `match` attribute when `type`==`match`

vlan_id.description: Identifier of the VLAN associated with this WxLAN tag when `type`==`vlan`

wxlan_tag_last_ips.description: Most recent IP addresses observed for a WxLAN tag

wxlan_tag_operation.description: required if `type`==`match`, type of tag (inclusive/exclusive). enum: `in`, `not_in`

wxlan_tag_spec.description: Traffic match specification used by a WxLAN tag

protocol.description: tcp / udp / icmp / gre / any / ":protocol_number", `protocol_number` is between 1-254

subnets.description: Destination subnets or IP addresses matched by this WxLAN tag spec

wxlan_tag_spec_subnets.description: Matched destination subnets and/or IP addresses

wxlan_tag_values.description:
Required if `type`==`match` and
  * `match`==`ap_id`: list of AP IDs
  * `match`==`app`: list of Application Names
  * `match`==`asset_mac`: list of Asset MAC addresses
  * `match`==`client_mac`: list of Client MAC addresses
  * `match`==`hostname`: list of Resources Hostnames
  * `match`==`ip_range_subnet`: list of IP addresses and/or CIDRs
  * `match`==`psk_name`: list of PSK Names
  * `match`==`psk_role`: list of PSK Roles
  * `match`==`port`: list of Ports or Port Ranges
  * `match`==`radius_attr`: list of RADIUS Attributes. The values are [ "6=1", "26=10.2.3.4" ], this support other RADIUS attributes where we know the type
  * `match`==`radius_class`: list of RADIUS Classes. This matches the ATTR-Class(25)
  * `match`==`radius_group`: list of RADIUS Groups. This is a smart tag that matches RADIUS-Filter-ID, Airespace-ACL-Name (VendorID=14179, VendorType=6) / Aruba-User-Role (VendorID=14823, VendorType=1)
  * `match`==`radius_username`: list of RADIUS Usernames. This matches the ATTR-User-Name(1)
  * `match`==`sdkclient_uuid`: list of SDK UUIDs
  * `match`==`wlan_id`: list of WLAN IDs

**Notes**:
Variables are not allowed

wxlan_tunnel.description: WxLAN tunnel configuration for L2TPv3 or DMVPN connectivity

created_time.description: Time when this WxLAN tunnel was created, in epoch seconds

dmvpn.description: Settings for Dynamic Multipoint VPN on this WxLAN tunnel

for_mgmt.description: Determined during creation time and cannot be toggled. A management tunnel cannot be used by wxlan rule or by wlan

hostname.description:
Optional, overwrite the hostname in SCCRQ control message, default is or null, %H and %M can be used, which will be replace with corresponding values:
  * %H: name of the ap if provided (and will be stripped so it can be used for hostname) and fallbacks to MAC
  * %M: MAC (e.g. 5c5b350e0060)

ipsec.description: Security settings for IPsec on this WxLAN tunnel; requires DMVPN to be enabled

is_static.description: Whether it’s static/unmanaged (i.e. no control session). As the session configurations are not compatible, cannot be toggled.

mtu.description: 0 to enable PMTU, 552-1500 to start PMTU with a lower MTU

name.description: The name of the tunnel

org_id.description: Owning organization associated with this WxLAN tunnel

peers.description: Remote peer IP addresses or hostnames for this tunnel

router_id.description: Optional, overwrite the router-id in SCCRQ control message, default is "" or null, can also be an IPv4 address

secret.description: Shared secret used for tunnel authentication, ‘’ if no auth is used

sessions.description: L2TPv3 sessions established for this tunnel

site_id.description: Mist site associated with this WxLAN tunnel, when site-scoped

use_udp.description: Whether to use UDP instead of IP (proto=115, which is default of L2TPv3)

enabled.description: Whether DMVPN is enabled

holding_time.description: Optional; the holding time for NHRP ‘registration requests’  and ‘resolution replies’ sent from the Mist AP (in seconds); default 600

host_routes.description: IPv4 DMVPN peer host routes to which traffic is forwarded

wxlan_tunnel_dmvpn_host_routes.description: Optional; list of IPv4 DMVPN peer host ip-addresses to which traffic is forwarded

wxlan_tunnel_ipsec.description: IPSec-related configurations; requires DMVPN be enabled

enabled.description: Whether ipsec is enabled, requires DMVPN be enabled

psk.description: Pre-shared key used for IPsec on this WxLAN tunnel

wxlan_tunnel_peers.description: List of remote peers’ IP or hostname

wxlan_tunnel_session.description: L2TPv3 session established inside a WxLAN tunnel

ap_as_session_id.description: If `use_ap_as_session_ids`==`true`, only apmac is supported right now. This is the name WLAN should use for wxtunnel_remote_id

comment.description: Optional, user-specified string for display purpose

enable_cookie.description: Whether L2TPv3 cookie support is enabled for this session

ethertype.description: Frame type carried by this tunnel session

local_session_id.description: Identifier for the local L2TPv3 session, from 1 to 2147483647

pseudo_802.1ad_enabled.description: Optional. Enables the pseudo 802.1ad QinQ mode where the AP device drops the outer vlan tag (QinQ). This mode is useful when tunneling Mist AP’s to some aggregation routers.

remote_id.description: Remote-id of the session, has to be unique in the same tunnel

use_ap_as_session_ids.description: Whether to use AP (last 4 bytes of MAC currently) as session ids

wxlan_tunnel_session_ethertype.description: Frame type carried by this tunnel session. enum: `ethernet`, `vlan`

wxlan_tunnel_sessions.description: Sessions to be established with the tunnel. Has to be >= 1 in order for this tunnel to be useful. For management tunnel, it can only have 1

apiToken.description:
Preferred authentication method for automation and integrations. Send the API token in the HTTP `Authorization` header.

**Format**:
  `Authorization: Token {apitoken}`

**Notes**:
* An API token generated for a specific admin has the same privileges as that admin
* An API token is automatically removed if it is not used for more than 90 days
* SSO admins cannot generate admin API tokens. Use organization API tokens when scoped Org/Site privileges are needed.
apiToken.name: Authorization

csrfToken.description:
Session-based authentication for browser or login/password flows. After a successful [Login](/#operations/login) request, Mist returns a `csrftoken` cookie. Send that value in the `X-CSRFToken` header on later API requests that use the login session.

**Format**:
```
X-CSRFToken: vwvBuq9qkqaKh7lu8tNc0gkvBfEaLAmx
```

For automation, API Token authentication is preferred.
csrfToken.name: X-CSRFToken
